results.text:
The Industrial Sales Model Wasn't Built for Data Centers

Manufacturing

# The Industrial Sales Model Wasn't Built for Data Centers

Author

Author

Author

## Why the old rules don’t apply and how to adapt GTM models for how the market buys

The data center market has quickly become a defining growth opportunity for today’s industrial suppliers. Total data center equipment and infrastructure spending is projected to approach $1 trillion by the end of the decade.[1] The facility infrastructure layer most relevant to industrial suppliers (e.g., power, thermal management, connectivity, and enclosures) is growing at double-digit rates, with power equipment reaching approximately $23 billion and cooling adding another $26 billion in 2025.[2]

However, many suppliers are finding that market participation and product fit aren’t enough to turn demand into revenue growth. At the core of the issue is a mismatch between how the data center market buys and how most industrial commercial models are built to sell. Drawing from Alexander Group’s research and project work in manufacturing and distribution, let’s examine what makes the data center market commercially distinct and what commercial leaders should do about it.

### Market Leaders Are Already Repositioning

Leading suppliers are consistently signaling how important data centers are becoming. One leading supplier reported data centers as its biggest growth driver, accounting for 30% of orders in 2025. Another

results.text:
OEM Rack PDU Solutions: Smart Power Distribution Unit for Global Data Centers - Geteknet-Network Cabling Solution Manufacturer

# OEM Rack PDU Solutions: Smart Power Distribution Unit for Global Data Centers

## What Is a PDU?

A Power Distribution Unit (PDU) is a critical device for distributing reliable electricity to multiple IT or telecom equipment within a rack. From basic rack PDU to intelligent PDU, As an experienced OEM manufacturer and supplier, GETEKnet provide a wide range of options to meet data center and enterprise power needs.

## Major Types of PDUs

Basic PDUs deliver straightforward power distribution.

Metered PDUs show real-time load on a display.

Switched or Managed PDUs allow remote outlet control.

Intelligent PDUs (Smart PDUs) integrate monitoring, metering, and network communication for advanced power management.

## Intelligent PDU Features

Smart PDUs support outlet-level metering, environmental sensors (temperature, humidity, airflow), and remote management through SNMP or REST API. These functions enable energy efficiency, capacity planning, and secure power distribution across global facilities.

## Plug Types and International Standards

Rack PDUs are available with a variety of plug and socket combinations to match local requirements. Besides the common IEC C13/C14/C19/C20 outlets, OEM PDUs can be built with:

NEMA connectors for the U.S. and Canada (e.g. NEMA 5-15R, 5-20R, L6-30R).

Schuko (Type F) “German” sockets widely used across Europe.

Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit (PDU) Market Size ($8.4 Billion) 2030

Home » Communication Networks And Infrastructure» Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit (PDU) Market Report 2030

- Table of Contents
- Report Description
- Contact

## Introduction And Strategic Context

The Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit (PDU) Market is poised for substantial growth in the coming years, driven by the increasing demand for data centers and the rising need for efficient power management solutions. The market, valued at USD 5.3 billion in 2024 , is projected to reach USD 8.4 billion by 2030 , experiencing a CAGR of 8.9% during the forecast period from 2024 to 2030, confirms Strategic Market Research.

Data centers are integral to modern digital infrastructure, supporting industries ranging from cloud computing and artificial intelligence (AI) to banking, telecommunications, and e-commerce. As the digital transformation accelerates, the need for high-performance, reliable, and efficient power distribution becomes critical. Rack PDUs are central to maintaining this power integrity, ensuring that servers, networking equipment, and storage systems receive the required voltage and current.

Several macro forces are influencing this market. The global shift toward cloud-based services and big data analytics is driving the expansion of data centers, both in established and emerging markets. Additionally, the growing demand for high-density computing, along with the increasing

results.title: Top PDU Manufacturers for Data Centers: Complete Guide
results.text:
---
description: Discover top PDU manufacturers for data centers. Compare leading brands, PDU types, and key selection criteria to power your infrastructure reliably.
---

May 15, 2026 

![Top PDU Manufacturers for Data Centers: Complete Guide](https://file-host.link/website/deipower-e6mdmu/assets/refined-images/1779989605893000_edad54d519f04029b0860d50869ff48e/360.webp)

## Introduction

NVIDIA's flagship NVL72 AI racks demand **132 kW per rack**. That number puts enormous pressure on the equipment distributing power inside data center cabinets — elevating PDUs from commodity hardware to mission-critical infrastructure. According to a [Vertiv summary of Uptime Institute's 2025 Global Data Center Survey](https://www.vertiv.com/en-in/insights/articles/blog-posts/must-read-research-its-the-data-center-economy-stupid-or-is-it/), mean rack density reached **7.6 kW in 2025**, up from 6.8 kW the year before.

Choosing the wrong PDU manufacturer creates real consequences: insufficient capacity, failed code inspections, costly field modifications, and monitoring blind spots.

This guide helps data center operators, electrical engineers, and contractors identify the right PDU partners based on [power density requirements](/blog/high-density-power-distribution-units-data-center-scalability), certifications, monitoring capabilities, and long-term scalability.

---

## Key Takeaways

* PDUs distribute electrical power from upstream sources to IT equipment within racks
* Top manufacturers
